# PrecodeOS AI Navigation Index

AUTHORITY: Curated AI-readable navigation index for stable PrecodeOS package documentation.
NOT_AUTHORITY: Active memory, task selection, PRD approval, bead activation, generated evidence, implementation status, package update behavior, runtime integration, or a replacement for canonical Markdown owner files.
LOAD_WHEN: An AI assistant or documentation tool needs a compact starting index for stable PrecodeOS docs and protocols before choosing the smallest relevant source.
CLASS: reference-navigation

Creator: Dan Sears / Recode
License: Apache-2.0
Copyright: (c) 2026 Dan Sears / Recode
Document version: v0.1.18
Last updated: 2026-07-11

This file is a navigation aid only. It points to canonical Markdown files and must not be used to approve work, choose tasks, run commands, replace active memory, summarize away source evidence, or infer current project state.

Use conditional advanced-surface routing. Advanced surfaces such as Review Lanes, Release Readiness, Goal Frames, Ralph, Attribution, Hypothesis Review, Plan Loop, Build-React-Learn, Artifact Chooser, team coordination, reversal, and proof tracing are available when needed. Do not present them as peer routes for the first-product spine or normal every-bead rhythm unless the current stage, risk, support role, recovery path, or explicit user question calls for one.
Use Skill Playbook Ergonomics when a user is unsure which skill-style prompt to invoke. It maps the request to Ask Precode, Workflow Selection, Ideation, Review / Acceptance, Skill / Extension Review, a normal owner protocol, a prompt-pattern entry, an adapter note, a script/check, or no new surface. It is not a skill catalog, registry, optional pack, command wrapper, approval gate, implementation path, or replacement for owner protocols.

Use the Plan Mode Candidate Craft Loop when candidate or implementation-plan commitment is at issue: `Idea -> Plan Mode -> Candidate Queue -> Plan Mode -> Implementation Plan -> Approved Bead -> Build`. In Codex use `/plan`, in Claude Code use Plan Mode, and in other agents use an equivalent read-only planning mode. Plan Mode is required before developing a Candidate Queue entry and before developing an implementation plan for a selected candidate; neither output authorizes implementation.
Use Question-To-Artifact Filing when a valuable answer from chat, planning, review, discovery, source intake, memory recall, or maintainer analysis should survive but its destination is unclear. It recommends the smallest existing destination: stay in chat, Local Source Intake, Candidate Queue, Memory Promotion Review, PRD draft or amendment, `DECISIONS.md`, owner-file update, decomposition review, defer, kill, or maintainer roadmap note. It does not file automatically, approve promotion, choose tasks, approve PRDs, activate beads, update active memory, create generated reports, or make generated summaries authoritative.
Use Source-To-Promotion Hygiene Review before turning a source summary, Candidate Queue entry, memory claim, or durable chat analysis into authority. It checks source refs, evidence strength, open conflicts, proposed owner, promotion action, approval required, and stop condition; it does not promote files, approve PRDs, choose tasks, update `tasks/todo.md`, or activate beads.

## Start Here

- `README.md`: public package compass, usage fit, setup path, and high-level navigation.
- `docs/PRECODE-GUIDED-SETUP.md`: setup-only adoption path before normal work starts.
- `docs/PRECODE-DAILY-COCKPIT.md`: beginner-facing operating home base, rough-idea entry prompt, first-product spine, and safe next prompts once PrecodeOS is installed or in use; also covers the every-bead rhythm and conditional advanced-surface routing.
- `docs/PRECODE-OS-README.md`: conceptual Builder OS explainer, not the daily start page.
- `docs/PRECODE-USER-GUIDE.md`: deeper operating manual behind the Daily Cockpit, not a second start page.
- `docs/PRECODE-PACKAGE-FILE-INVENTORY.md`: public package file map, context-layer matrix, authority boundaries, and ownership dictionary.
- `CANDIDATE-QUEUE.md`: parked candidate intent, product-value ratings, themes, near-bead sketches, and promotion review surface; not task authority.

## Active Memory Contract

Normal Precode sessions start from exactly:

- `AGENT.md`
- `DECISIONS.md`
- `tasks/todo.md`

Do not expand active memory through this index, generated reports, reviewed memory, adapters, skill playbooks, or maintainer-local files.

## Protocol Entry Points

- `tasks/reference/WORKFLOW-SELECTION-PROTOCOL.md`: choose the right Precode workflow before acting, including First PRD Walkthrough as the rough-idea route, `Idea -> Brief -> Packet -> Intake -> PRD -> Bead -> Proof -> Review -> Close` as the visible first-product spine, Plan Mode Candidate Craft Loop gates before candidate or implementation-plan commitment, and Plan Loop routing for explicit post-intake or post-PRD feature-angle exploration before bead commitment.
- `tasks/prds/PRD-036-task-suitability-split-heuristics.md`: advisory task-suitability and split-readiness guidance for deciding whether current work should continue, clarify, route, split, block, or stop before implementation.
- `tasks/reference/CANDIDATE-QUEUE-PROTOCOL.md`: park ideas, review candidate intent, assign product-value ratings, group themes, sketch near-beads, route candidates to intake/discovery/PRD/decomposition, and preserve non-authority boundaries.
- `tasks/reference/EXTENSION-PROTOCOL.md`: review adapters, protocols, skill playbooks, generated reports, wrappers, indexes, and integrations.
- `tasks/reference/EXTERNAL-STATUS-INTEGRATION-PROTOCOL.md`: provider-neutral external status, safe health URL, missing-configuration, and evidence-only promotion rules.
- `tasks/reference/SKILL-PLAYBOOK-PROTOCOL.md`: read-only prompt playbooks for host agents, including Skill Playbook Ergonomics decision guidance that keeps beginner invocation behind existing workflow moments.
- `tasks/reference/MEMORY-PROTOCOL.md`: reviewed memory search, citation, freshness, and promotion rules.
- `tasks/reference/TOOL-EXECUTION-PROTOCOL.md`: tool-call classes, command approval, failure categories, and proof-vs-tool-use boundaries.
- `tasks/reference/ENGINEERING-QUALITY-STANDARDS-PROTOCOL.md`: thin pre-coding engineering quality floor, beginner-readable Engineering Quality Standards Taxonomy, optional Engineering Quality Text-Contract Checker routing, post-implementation Engineering Quality Review Lane routing, and proportional routing when implementation risk appears.
- `tasks/prds/PRD-040-release-readiness-quality-expansion.md`: release-quality cues inside existing Release Readiness and advisory completion-check output; not a new checker, release gate, production-readiness certification, provider checklist, deployment automation, release-channel behavior, or package-manager behavior.
- `tasks/prds/PRD-038-engineering-quality-review-lane.md`: advisory Engineering Quality Review Lane requirements for checking completed or nearly completed active beads against the quality floor; not implementation acceptance, code-quality certification, production-readiness certification, scorecard authority, checker gate, app-code parsing, repo heuristics, language-aware analysis, follow-up task creation, or owner-file rewrite.
- `tasks/prds/PRD-039-engineering-quality-repo-heuristics.md`: advisory Engineering Quality Repo Heuristics Preview requirements for explicit `--repo-heuristics-preview` changed-file repo-shape warnings; not default checker behavior, app-code parsing, AST analysis, linter replacement, test execution, code-quality scoring, implementation approval, review acceptance, or checker-gate authority.
- `tasks/prds/PRD-035-afk-delegation-safety-improvements.md`: bounded AFK and delegation safety requirements; AFK metadata is not autonomous execution, command, review, merge, or transition approval.
- `tasks/prds/PRD-037-delegation-re-entry-evidence-pack.md`: delegated return evidence requirements for solo AFK, branch/worktree teammate, and cloud-agent/PR returns; re-entry fields are evidence only and do not approve continue, acceptance, merge, transition, GitHub mutation, or external mutation.
- `tasks/reference/VERIFICATION-GUARDRAIL-PROTOCOL.md`: proof expectations and generated-evidence limits.
- `ACCEPTANCE.md` and `tasks/reference/PRD-PROTOCOL.md`: acceptance criteria and PRD acceptance-oracle guidance, including optional EARS-style wording for vague expected behavior.
- `tasks/reference/SESSION-COMPLETION-HANDOFF-PROTOCOL.md`: closeout, handoff, and review evidence shape.
- `tasks/reference/RECOVERY-PROTOCOL.md`: stuck-user recovery and safe triage.

## Stable Help Surfaces

- `docs/PRECODE-GUIDED-SETUP.md`: beginner-safe package adoption guidance.
- `docs/PRECODE-TROUBLESHOOTING.md`: symptom-first recovery and validation routing.
- `docs/PRECODE-SUPPORT-RUNBOOK.md`: support-assisted setup and recovery guidance.
- `docs/CLAUDE-CODE-FIELD-GUIDE.md`: public field guide for Claude Code users.
- `tasks/reference/PROMPT-PATTERNS.md`: copyable prompts for common Precode workflows.

## Generated Reading Surfaces

- `docs-html/*.html`: generated public reading surface for `docs/*.md` and selected root public docs, including `CANDIDATE-QUEUE.md`, with static progress cues, section links, and source Markdown links; Markdown remains canonical.
- `scripts/docs-html.py --check`: public freshness check for `docs-html/*.html`.
- `scripts/engineering-quality-check.py`: Engineering Quality Text-Contract Checker; advisory only, including package text-contract coverage for Engineering Quality Review Lane references and explicit `--repo-heuristics-preview` changed-file repo-shape warnings; not implementation approval, review acceptance, generated proof, app-code parsing, AST analysis, linter replacement, test execution, scorecard, or checker gate.
- `scripts/completion-check.py`: advisory completion and release-evidence readiness output, including release-quality cue warnings through `details.release_evidence`; not release approval, production-readiness certification, generated proof, deployment automation, or a checker gate.
- `scripts/package-knowledge-lint.py`: Package Knowledge Lint over the public package core; advisory generated evidence only for broken internal links or anchors, duplicate heading labels, orphan public references, stale generated sidecar cues, duplicate authority claims, and public/private boundary risk; not edit approval, task selection, source promotion, automatic doc rewrite, stale-claim authority, proof, implementation acceptance, checker gate, registry, optional-pack behavior, install/update behavior, release-channel behavior, or package-manager behavior.
- `scripts/external-status.py`: provider-neutral read-only external status evidence for GitHub, safe health URLs from `PROJECT-CONTEXT.md`, and configured-provider absence; not proof, approval, deployment, merge, release, owner-file mutation, dashboard mutation, external mutation, registry, optional-pack behavior, install/update behavior, or package-manager behavior.
- `scripts/task-suitability-check.py`: Task Suitability Checker; advisory continue/clarify/route/split/block/stop guidance only, not task selection, PRD approval, bead activation, implementation authorization, review acceptance, command approval, or generated proof.
- `scripts/candidate-queue.py`: local Candidate Queue preview/apply helper; preview is non-authority and apply requires explicit `--approve-action`.
- `scripts/session-friction-check.py`: read-only Session Friction Review diagnostic; findings are generated evidence only and require manual promotion.
- `scripts/build-attribution-ledger.py`: read-only Build Attribution Ledger diagnostic; who-built-what findings are generated evidence only and require closeout review.
- `scripts/prd-handoff-readiness.py`: read-only PRD Handoff Readiness Packet diagnostic; readiness packets are generated evidence only and do not approve PRDs or activate beads.
- `tasks/prds-html/*.html`: generated PRD review surface with PRD handoff readiness cues and Acceptance Oracle export controls; Markdown PRDs remain canonical.
- `OS-HEALTH.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, and `logs/*`: generated evidence only, not authority.

## Maintainer Context

Private maintainer material under `_maintainer/` is not normal user-session input and is not public package authority. Use it only for Dan's package-maintenance work when explicitly requested.

## Guardrails

- Do not install, wrap, proxy, compress, update, or mutate any runtime from this index.
- Do not use this file as a package registry, optional-pack manifest, or tool integration contract.
- Do not summarize raw source evidence in a way that replaces owner files, PRDs, beads, recorded checks, logs, or manual verification.
- When a question depends on current state, inspect active memory, the active bead, source Markdown, generated evidence, or the relevant owner file directly.
